changes.result: On the left window pane, scroll down and select **Network** to view devices on your network and identify a device.
Right-click on the device and select **Properties** to obtain the device's IP address information.
View the Zebra Web HMI and observe the build number RELEASE-xx (where xx is the build number) in the **Device Information** section of the dashboard.
Click **Operator** to launch the login window.
Click **Login**.
Observe that **Operator** now displays as **Admin**.

On the **General** tab, click **Reset All** in the **Factory Reset** field, and wait for the device to reboot (approximately 45-60 seconds).
Select the **Data Formatting** tab to access **Advanced Filtering and Formatting**.
Select the **Advanced Filtering and Formatting** radio button.
Click **Edit Rules** to create a new rule or edit an existing rule. Rename the rule if necessary.

Click **Add Group** to add a parenthesis block that contains rules separated by the same combinator.
To add an **Action** , click **Add**.
Click to specify an **Action** to add to the list of selected actions by clicking the plus sign.
After a **Filter** and **Action** are identified for the rule, and rules are managed by clicking the ellipses.
The **Build** tab facilitates job configuration and deployment for FIS and MV tools.
To configure **Custom Formatting** , access the **Data Formatting** tab from the **Build** tab.
Select the **Custom Formatting** radio button.
Click next to **Find** to add a data type to identify.
Next, click next to **Replace With** to designate a data type to replace the data type identified in the Find form field.
Click next to the **Prefix** form field to add a data type. Follow the same steps for **Data** and **Suffix.**
Select a **Data Type Delimiter** from the drop-down.
Select a **End Delimiter** from the drop-down.
Click **Get Last** to retrieve the **String** or **Result**.
Click **Deploy** while in **Edit** mode
to enter **Deploy** mode
Ensure that **Enable TCP/IP Control** and **Enable TCP/IP Results** are enabled in **Communication** settings.
Add the tool in **FlowBuilder** using the **Build** tab to view match string options.
Select the **Barcode String Match** mode from the drop-down menu.
Click **Last Decode** to use the last successful read string as the current match string value.
In Aurora Focus, navigate to the **Communication** section in **Device Settings**.
Click **Enable SSI communication over USB** under **Automation Wedge** in the **USB Settings** section.

It is recommended to align the image name with the setting name in **Acquisition Settings**.

Click **Control Device** to access controls to the Modbus device.
Select the Modbus device from the menu and click **Start** to begin controlling the device. Click **Stop** to end the session or **Delete** to remove
Click **Results** to access Modbus results and configure where results are sent to.
Select the Modbus device from the menu and define a **Holding Register**.
Click **Confirm** to save changes and return to the **Admin Settings** menu.
Click **Create Device** to configure the device information.
Provide the required device information and click **Create** to return to the **Admin Settings** menu.
Click **Monitoring** to monitor a specific coil on the Modbus device

Navigate to the **Configuration** screen using the left menu on the Home screen.
Locate the **Serial** section and click **Config** to set the serial settings for the device.
Configure the form fields and click **Confirm** to enable the settings on the device.
Access **Network Config** from the **Admin Settings** menu to edit the hostname and server settings and click **Update** to save each setting.
Navigate to the **Setup** screen using the left menu on the **Home** screen.

Click **Update** to enable the changes and update the field values. Select **Server** to use the TCP port as a server.

from the **Admin Settings** menu.

3. 3. Complete the job configuration and click **Capture** tab and navigate to the **Triggers** tab to set the **Trigger Source** to **Serial.**
4. 4. Click **Connect** tab and navigate to the **Triggers** tab.
Ensure the **Trigger Source** is set to **TCP/IP**.
Check the **Enable RS-232 Control** checkbox (disabled by default).
Observe the **Trigger String** and keep this value for reference to send a Serial trigger from external software.
Click **Apply** in the RS-232 area and **OK** on the resulting prompt to save the configuration.
Make note of the **Control Part Number** and **Trigger String** fields. This information is required to send a TCP/IP trigger from external software.
Click **Apply** in the TCP/IP settings window. Click **OK** in the following prompt.
Click **Open** to open a serial connection in terminal.
Scroll down to **RS-232 Settings**.
Click **Apply** within the **RS-232 Settings** area.
7. 7. Enter **Serial** tab.
Click **Open** to establish a serial connection.
Type the **Trigger String** as defined in **Settings** in Zebra Aurora Focus and click **Enter.**
Enter **TRIGGER** as the default
and click **Send** to send a trigger using serial.
Disable the **Enable DHCP** checkbox.
Click **Apply** when complete. The device reboots with the new static IP address.
Scroll to **USB Settings**.
Click **Apply** within the USB Settings.
Click **OK**.
